evals.prompt: I'm getting a Yjs warning in the console about importing Yjs twice. We're using Next.js and I think our bundler might be including more than one version. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Instructions to fix duplicate Yjs imports: either package manager resolution (resolutions/overrides in package.json for npm/yarn/pnpm) or bundler config (aliases/resolve) so only one Yjs instance is used. Mention that duplicate imports break class reference sharing and cause sync issues.
assertions: The response mentions package manager resolution (resolutions, overrides, or equivalent) or bundler configuration
The response does not suggest importing Yjs in a different file as the fix

evals.prompt: We have a Y.Map that we use for key-value storage. We're updating different keys in alternating order (key1, key2, key1, key2...) and the document size is huge. What should we use instead?
evals.expected_output: Recommend YKeyValue from y-utility for efficient key-value storage. Explain that Y.Map retains history for conflict resolution and grows with alternating writes; YKeyValue keeps document size proportional to map size, not operation count.
assertions: The response recommends YKeyValue or y-utility/y-keyvalue
The response includes or references how to install or import (e.g. npm install y-utility)

evals.prompt: We want to put multiple rich text editors on the same page, all synced with Liveblocks. Should we use Yjs subdocuments for each editor?
evals.expected_output: Recommend avoiding subdocuments for multiple editors on the same page. Suggest using a single Y.Doc with a Y.Map (e.g. 'editors') and storing Y.XMLFragment (or similar) per editor in the map. Subdocuments are for very large docs or lazy-loading needs.
assertions: The response says subdocuments are not necessary for multiple editors on the same page
The response suggests using a Y.Map to organize editors (or equivalent structure in one doc)
The response mentions when subdocuments are appropriate (e.g. very large docs, lazy-loading)

evals.prompt: We're using getYjsProviderForRoom with Liveblocks. How can we get smaller document sizes and better performance when using maps?
evals.expected_output: Recommend enabling experimental V2 encoding: useV2Encoding_experimental: true in getYjsProviderForRoom options. Note that all clients must use the same options.
assertions: The response mentions useV2Encoding_experimental or V2 encoding
The response states that all clients must have the same options (or equivalent caveat)
